children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The term “eligible project” means a project to replace, rehabilitate, preserve, or protect 1 or more bridges on the National Bridge Inventory under section 144(b).

children.label: i
children.text: a bundle of projects described in subparagraph (A), regardless of whether the bundle of projects meets the requirements of section 144(j)(5); and

children.label: ii
children.text: a project to replace or rehabilitate culverts for the purpose of improving flood control and improved habitat connectivity for aquatic species.

children.heading: .—Large project
children.label: 2 .—Large project
children.text: The term “large project” means an eligible project with total eligible project costs of greater than $100,000,000.

children.heading: .—Program
children.label: 3 .—Program
children.text: The term “program” means the bridge investment program established by subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Establishment of Bridge Investment Program.—
children.label: b Establishment of Bridge Investment Program.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: There is established a bridge investment program to provide financial assistance for eligible projects under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: to improve the safety, efficiency, and reliability of the movement of people and freight over bridges;

children.label: I
children.text: in poor condition; or

children.label: II
children.text: in fair condition and at risk of falling into poor condition within the next 3 years;

children.label: I
children.text: in poor condition; or

children.label: II
children.text: in fair condition and at risk of falling into poor condition within the next 3 years;

children.label: I
children.text: do not meet current geometric design standards; or

children.label: II
children.text: cannot meet the load and traffic requirements typical of the regional transportation network; and

children.label: I

children.label: II
children.text: cannot meet the load and traffic requirements typical of the regional transportation network; and

children.label: C
children.text: to provide financial assistance that leverages and encourages non-Federal contributions from sponsors and stakeholders involved in the planning, design, and construction of eligible projects.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: In carrying out the program, the Secretary may award grants, on a competitive basis, in accordance with this section.

children.label: i

children.label: ii

children.label: ii

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a large project, not more than 50 percent; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any other eligible project, not more than 80 percent.

children.heading: .—Maximum federal involvement
children.label: A .—Maximum federal involvement
children.text: Federal assistance other than a grant under the program may be used to satisfy the non-Federal share of the cost of a project for which a grant is made, except that the total Federal assistance provided for a project receiving a grant under the program may not exceed the Federal share for the project under section 120.

children.label: i
children.text: Federal assistance other than a grant under the program may be used to satisfy the non-Federal share of the cost of a project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the total Federal assistance provided for the project shall not exceed 90 percent of the total eligible project costs.

children.heading: .—Federal land management agencies and tribal governments
children.label: C .—Federal land management agencies and tribal governments
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, Federal funds other than Federal funds made available under this section may be used to pay the remaining share of the cost of a project under the program by a Federal land management agency or a Tribal government or consortium of Tribal governments.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a large project, the ratings assigned under subsection (g)(5)(A);

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an eligible project other than a large project, the quality rating assigned under subsection (f)(3)(A)(ii);

children.label: iii
children.text: the average daily person and freight throughput supported by the eligible project;

children.label: iv
children.text: the number and percentage of bridges within the same State as the eligible project that are in poor condition;

children.label: v
children.text: the extent to which the eligible project demonstrates cost savings by bundling multiple bridge projects;

children.label: vi
children.text: in the case of an eligible project of a Federal land management agency, the extent to which the grant would reduce a Federal liability or Federal infrastructure maintenance backlog;

children.label: vii
children.text: geographic diversity among grant recipients, including the need for a balance between the needs of rural and urban communities; and

children.label: I
children.text: 2 or more applications for eligible projects within the State were submitted for the current fiscal year and an average of 2 or more applications for eligible projects within the State were submitted in prior fiscal years of the program; and

children.label: II
children.text: fewer than 2 grants have been awarded for eligible projects within the State under the program;

children.label: I
children.text: not fewer than 1 large project that the Secretary determines is justified under the evaluation under subsection (g)(4); or

children.label: II
children.text: 2 eligible projects that are not large projects that the Secretary determines are justified under the evaluation under subsection (f)(3); and

children.label: iii
children.text: not be required to award a grant for an eligible project that the Secretary does not determine is justified under an evaluation under subsection (f)(3) or (g)(4).

children.heading: .—Culvert limitation
children.label: 6 .—Culvert limitation
children.text: Not more than 5 percent of the amounts made available for each fiscal year for grants under the program may be used for eligible projects that consist solely of culvert replacement or rehabilitation.
A State or a group of States.
A metropolitan planning organization that serves an urbanized area (as designated by the Bureau of the Census) with a population of over 200,000.
A unit of local government or a group of local governments.
A political subdivision of a State or local government.
A special purpose district or public authority with a transportation function.
A Federal land management agency.
A Tribal government or a consortium of Tribal governments.
A multistate or multijurisdictional group of entities described in paragraphs (1) through (7).
in the case of a large project, the Secretary recommends for funding in the annual report on funding recommendations under subsection (g)(6), except as provided in subsection (g)(1)(B);
is reasonably expected to begin construction not later than 18 months after the date on which funds are obligated for the project; and
is based on the results of preliminary engineering.

children.heading: Competitive Process and Evaluation of Eligible Projects Other Than Large Projects.—
children.label: f Competitive Process and Evaluation of Eligible Projects Other Than Large Projects.—
i
children.text: for the first fiscal year for which funds are made available for obligation under the program, not later than 60 days after the date on which the template under subparagraph (B)(i) is developed, and in subsequent fiscal years, not later than 60 days after the date on which amounts are made available for obligation under the program, solicit grant applications for eligible projects other than large projects; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not later than 120 days after the date on which the solicitation under clause (i) expires, conduct evaluations under paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: develop a template for applicants to use to summarize project needs and benefits, including benefits described in paragraph (3)(B)(i); and

children.label: ii
children.text: enable applicants to use data from the National Bridge Inventory under section 144(b) to populate templates described in clause (i), as applicable.

children.heading: .—Applications
children.label: 2 .—Applications
children.text: An eligible entity shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: i
children.text: conduct an evaluation of each eligible project for which an application is received under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: assign a quality rating to the eligible project on the basis of the evaluation under clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: costs avoided by the prevention of closure or reduced use of the bridge to be improved by the project;

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a bundle of projects, benefits from executing the projects as a bundle compared to as individual projects;

children.label: III
children.text: safety benefits, including the reduction of accidents and related costs;

children.label: IV
children.text: person and freight mobility benefits, including congestion reduction and reliability improvements;

children.label: V
children.text: national or regional economic benefits;

children.label: VI
children.text: benefits from long-term resiliency to extreme weather events, flooding, or other natural disasters;

children.label: VII
children.text: benefits from protection (as described in section 133(b)(10)), including improving seismic or scour protection;

children.label: VIII
children.text: environmental benefits, including wildlife connectivity;

children.label: IX
children.text: benefits to nonvehicular and public transportation users;

children.label: aa
children.text: innovative design and construction techniques; or

children.label: bb
children.text: innovative technologies; or

children.label: XI
children.text: reductions in maintenance costs, including, in the case of a federally-owned bridge, cost savings to the Federal budget; and

children.label: ii
children.text: consider whether and the extent to which the benefits, including the benefits described in clause (i), are more likely than not to outweigh the total project costs.

children.heading: Competitive Process, Evaluation, and Annual Report for Large Projects.—
children.label: g Competitive Process, Evaluation, and Annual Report for Large Projects.—

children.heading: .—Applications
children.label: A .—Applications
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an annual date by which an eligible entity submitting an application for a large project shall submit to the Secretary such information as the Secretary may require, including information described in paragraph (2), in order for a large project to be considered for a recommendation by the Secretary for funding in the next annual report under paragraph (6).

children.heading: .—First fiscal year
children.label: B .—First fiscal year
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), for the first fiscal year for which funds are made available for obligation for grants under the program, the Secretary may establish a date by which an eligible entity submitting an application for a large project shall submit to the Secretary such information as the Secretary may require, including information described in paragraph (2), in order for a large project to be considered for immediate execution of a grant agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: all necessary information required for the Secretary to evaluate the large project; and

children.label: i
children.text: the large project meets the applicable requirements under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: there is a reasonable likelihood that the large project will continue to meet the requirements under this section.

children.label: A

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: B .—Requirement
children.text: To be considered justified and receive a recommendation for funding in the annual report under paragraph (6), a project shall receive a rating of not less than “medium” for each rating required under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Annual report on funding recommendations for large projects.—
children.label: 6 Annual report on funding recommendations for large projects.—
i
children.text: a list of large projects that have requested a recommendation for funding under a new grant agreement from funds anticipated to be available to carry out this subsection in the next fiscal year;

children.label: ii

children.label: I
children.text: scheduled payments under previously signed multiyear grant agreements under subsection (j);

children.label: II
children.text: payments for new grant agreements, including single-year grant agreements and multiyear grant agreements; and

children.label: III
children.text: a description of how amounts anticipated to be available for the program from the Highway Trust Fund for that fiscal year will be distributed; and

children.label: iv
children.text: for each project for which the Secretary recommends a new multiyear grant agreement under subsection (j), the proposed payout schedule for the project.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall not recommend in an annual report under this paragraph a new multiyear grant agreement provided from funds from the Highway Trust Fund unless the Secretary determines that the project can be completed using funds that are anticipated to be available from the Highway Trust Fund in future fiscal years.

children.label: I
children.text: may recommend for funding in an annual report under this paragraph a large project using funds from the general fund of the Treasury; but

children.label: aa
children.text: funds other than from the Highway Trust Fund have been made available for the project; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the Secretary determines that the project can be completed using funds other than from the Highway Trust Fund that are anticipated to be available in future fiscal years.

children.label: i

children.label: ii

expenses related to the protection (as described in section 133(b)(10)) of a bridge, including seismic or scour protection.

children.heading: .—TIFIA Program
children.label: i .—TIFIA Program
children.text: On the request of an eligible entity carrying out an eligible project, the Secretary may use amounts awarded to the entity to pay subsidy and administrative costs necessary to provide to the entity Federal credit assistance under chapter 6 with respect to the eligible project for which the grant was awarded.

children.heading: Multiyear Grant Agreements for Large Projects.—
children.label: j Multiyear Grant Agreements for Large Projects.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A large project that receives a grant under the program in an amount of not less than $100,000,000 may be carried out through a multiyear grant agreement in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: establish the terms of participation by the Federal Government in the project;

children.label: B
children.text: establish the maximum amount of Federal financial assistance for the project in accordance with paragraphs (3) and (4) of subsection (c);

children.label: C
children.text: establish a payout schedule for the project that provides for disbursement of the full grant amount by not later than 4 fiscal years after the fiscal year in which the initial amount is provided;

children.label: D
children.text: determine the period of time for completing the project, even if that period extends beyond the period of an authorization; and

children.label: E
children.text: attempt to improve timely and efficient management of the project, consistent with all applicable Federal laws (including regulations).

children.label: i
children.text: shall obligate an amount of available budget authority specified in law; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may include a commitment, contingent on amounts to be specified in law in advance for commitments under this paragraph, to obligate an additional amount from future available budget authority specified in law.

children.heading: .—Statement of contingent commitment
children.label: B .—Statement of contingent commitment
children.text: The agreement shall state that the contingent commitment is not an obligation of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Interest and other financing costs.—
children.label: C Interest and other financing costs.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Interest and other financing costs of carrying out a part of the project within a reasonable time shall be considered a cost of carrying out the project under a multiyear grant agreement, except that eligible costs may not be more than the cost of the most favorable financing terms reasonably available for the project at the time of borrowing.

children.heading: .—Certification
children.label: ii .—Certification
children.text: The applicant shall certify to the Secretary that the applicant has shown reasonable diligence in seeking the most favorable financing terms.

children.label: A
children.text: may use funds made available to the eligible entity under this title for eligible project costs of the large project until the amount specified in the multiyear grant agreement for the project for that fiscal year becomes available for obligation; and

children.label: B
children.text: if the eligible entity uses funds as described in subparagraph (A), the funds used shall be reimbursed from the amount made available under the multiyear grant agreement for the project.

children.heading: Undertaking Parts of Projects in Advance Under Letters of No Prejudice.—
children.label: k Undertaking Parts of Projects in Advance Under Letters of No Prejudice.—
A
children.text: before the applicant carries out the activity, the Secretary approves through a letter to the applicant the activity in the same manner as the Secretary approves other activities as eligible under the program;

children.label: B
children.text: a record of decision, a finding of no significant impact, or a categorical exclusion under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) has been issued for the eligible project; and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: C
children.text: the activity is carried out without Federal assistance and in accordance with all applicable procedures and requirements.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the cost of carrying out an activity for an eligible project includes the amount of interest and other financing costs, including any interest earned and payable on bonds, to the extent interest and other financing costs are expended in carrying out the activity for the eligible project, except that interest and other financing costs may not be more than the cost of the most favorable financing terms reasonably available for the eligible project at the time of borrowing.

children.heading: .—Certification
children.label: B .—Certification
children.text: The applicant shall certify to the Secretary that the applicant has shown reasonable diligence in seeking the most favorable financing terms under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: constitute an obligation of the Federal Government; or

children.label: B
children.text: alter or influence any evaluation under subsection (f)(3)(A)(i) or (g)(4) or any recommendation by the Secretary for funding under the program.

children.label: A
children.text: shall consider options to divest the bridge to a State or local entity after completion of the project; and

children.label: B
children.text: may apply jointly with the State or local entity to which the bridge may be divested.

children.heading: .—Treatment
children.label: 2 .—Treatment
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, section 129 shall apply to a bridge that was previously owned by a Federal land management agency and has been transferred to a non-Federal entity under paragraph (1) in the same manner as if the bridge was never federally owned.

children.heading: .—Treatment of Projects
children.label: m .—Treatment of Projects
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a project assisted under this section shall be treated as a project on a Federal-aid highway under this chapter.
an evaluation and justification for the eligible project; and
the amount of the proposed grant.

children.heading: .—Annual report
children.label: 1 .—Annual report
children.text: Not later than August 1 of each fiscal year, the Secretary shall make available on the website of the Department of Transportation an annual report that lists each eligible project for which a grant has been provided under the program during the fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct an assessment of the administrative establishment, solicitation, selection, and justification process with respect to the funding of grants under the program; and

children.label: i
children.text: the adequacy and fairness of the process under which each eligible project that received a grant under the program was selected; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the justification and criteria used for the selection of each eligible project.

children.heading: .—Large projects
children.label: 1 .—Large projects
children.text: Of the amounts made available out of the Highway Trust Fund (other than the Mass Transit Account) to carry out this section for each of fiscal years 2022 through 2026, not less than 50 percent, in aggregate, shall be used for large projects.

children.heading: .—Unutilized amounts
children.label: 2 .—Unutilized amounts
children.text: If, in fiscal year 2026, the Secretary determines that grants under the program will not allow for the requirement under paragraph (1) to be met, the Secretary shall use the unutilized amounts to make other grants under the program during that fiscal year.

children.heading: Tribal Transportation Facility Bridge Set Aside.—
children.label: q Tribal Transportation Facility Bridge Set Aside.—
A
children.text: $16,000,000 for fiscal year 2022;

children.label: B
children.text: $18,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: C
children.text: $20,000,000 for fiscal year 2024;

children.label: D
children.text: $22,000,000 for fiscal year 2025; and

children.label: E
children.text: $24,000,000 for fiscal year 2026.

children.heading: .—Treatment
children.label: 2 .—Treatment
children.text: For purposes of section 201, funds made available for section 202(d) under paragraph (1) shall be considered to be part of the tribal transportation program.
